title: Review: Rich Push Notifications (ChatMessagePush, PushSubscription/set)
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — The correctness issues (items 1-4) are the real risk; none are exploitable for data exfiltration, but the encrypted-invariant gap and missing /set operations will generate user bug reports. The public API surface (PushSubscriptionSetResponse.created as Value, missing update/destroy) will fossilize immediately.
Confidence: 15% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) I have not seen the actual Web Push delivery layer — the client builds the subscription but nothing tests that the URL field is validated as https-only or that it survives server-side URL validation; (2) the interaction between chatPush and RFC 8620 update/verification flow (client secret, verificationCode) is entirely unimplemented and untested — the spec references RFC 8620 §7.2 which requires a verification round-trip that this implementation skips entirely. Would raise confidence if: full RFC 8620 §7.2 verification flow is implemented and tested; a test covers the empty chat_push case (no capability in using array); and the encrypted invariant enforcement is added.
